verses.text: The LORD to Aaron: thou and thy sons shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary; the priesthood is your gift. Bring also the Levites — they shall join thee and minister, but not come nigh the holy vessels lest they die. I have given the priest's office to you as a service of gift. Guidance: After Korah's gravestone is built into the altar, God redraws the lines. The priesthood is a gift, not a prize. Each calling carries its own weight — and its own boundary.

Bring also the Levites — they shall join thee and minister, but not come nigh the holy vessels lest they die. I have given the priest's office to you as a service of gift. Guidance: After Korah's gravestone is built into the altar, God redraws the lines. The priesthood is a gift, not a prize. Each calling carries its own weight — and its own boundary.

The LORD: I have given thee the heave offerings — the meat, sin, trespass offerings; the best of oil, wine, wheat. Every devoted thing in Israel is thine; every firstborn is thine. Redeem the firstborn of man and unclean beasts. All heave offerings are thy due — a covenant of salt forever. Guidance: God's table for His servants. The priest gets the best, not the leftovers. Salt does not spoil; the covenant does not expire. Those who serve at the altar live from the altar.

The LORD to Aaron: thou shalt have no inheritance in their land, neither part among them — I am thy part and thine inheritance. To the Levites I give the tithe in Israel for their service. Israel shall come no more near the tabernacle, lest they bear sin and die. Guidance: Aaron's portion is not real estate — it is God Himself. The poorest priest in Israel is the richest man, because his deed is the LORD. Those who have nothing else are not impoverished when they have Him.

The LORD: speak to the Levites — when ye take the tithe from Israel, offer up a tenth of the tithe to the LORD; give it to Aaron the priest. Reckon it as the corn of the threshingfloor. Bear no sin — pollute not the holy things. Guidance: Even the receivers must give. The Levites tithe what they were given. No one in God's house is exempt from offering. The cycle of grace flows through, not into.
